ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002383 covers hamburger as a broad class; SmokeShack is a specific branded variant with bacon, cherry peppers, and ShackSauce. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKW covers hamburger broadly; no branded Shake Shack SmokeShack entry exists. Closest defensible FoodEx2 code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No sufficiently specific SNOMED CT food concept identified for this branded burger product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One SmokeShack burger as served, approximately 220 g, comprising a beef patty, potato bun, American cheese, applewood-smoked bacon, cherry peppers, and ShackSauce.

nutrient_source.title: Shake Shack SmokeShack — USDA Branded Food (FDC ID 2341827)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA Branded Food entry for Shake Shack SmokeShack burger used as primary nutrient source. Values are per 220 g serving as declared; converted to per-100 g basis for this record. The SmokeShack is a commercially standardised product; USDA Branded Foods is the approved source for packaged and commercial products per Project Veritas source policy.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Shake Shack SmokeShack. GI estimated at 54 (low) based on the closest analogous food: hamburger on a white-flour bun, GI ~61 per University of Sydney GI Database, adjusted downward to ~54 to account for the high fat and protein content of this burger (fat and protein are known to attenuate postprandial glucose response). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (54 × 38.1) / 100 ≈ 20.6. Carbohydrate per serving = 17.3 g/100 g × 220 g = 38.1 g. Note that despite a low GI, the large serving size results in a high glycemic load.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (477 mg per 100 g; approximately 1050 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid the SmokeShack, as a single serving provides approximately 44–46% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by major cardiovascular guidelines, primarily from processed cheese, bacon, and ShackSauce.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic load (~20.6 per serving) combined with high saturated fat from bacon and cheese
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should consume the SmokeShack only occasionally, as the high glycemic load (~20.6 per serving) and high saturated fat content (approximately 14 g per serving) can impair postprandial glycaemic control and contribute to insulin resistance over time.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat, trans fat, sodium, and cholesterol from beef patty, bacon, and cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should treat the SmokeShack as an occasional food, given its combination of saturated fat (~14 g per serving), trans fat (~1.1 g per serving), cholesterol (~120 mg per serving), and high sodium (~1050 mg per serving), all of which are established risk factors for atherosclerosis and elevated LDL cholesterol.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The SmokeShack is a signature beef cheeseburger created by Shake Shack, an American fast-casual restaurant chain. It consists of a 100% Angus beef patty served on a toasted potato bun, topped with American cheese, crispy applewood-smoked bacon, spicy-tangy cherry peppers, and ShackSauce — a proprietary mayonnaise-based condiment with a mildly smoky and tangy flavour profile. The burger is grilled to order and assembled fresh. The flavour profile is savoury, smoky, and slightly spicy, with a rich umami depth from the beef and bacon, balanced by the acidity of the cherry peppers. Nutritionally, the SmokeShack is a calorie-dense, high-protein, high-fat food providing approximately 590 kcal per serving (220 g), with 32 g fat, 14 g saturated fat, and 1050 mg sodium. It is clinically relevant for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its high sodium, saturated fat, and high glycemic load. In Singapore, the SmokeShack is available at Shake Shack outlets including 89 Neil Road and One Fullerton, priced at approximately SGD 13.20. It is classified as a processed fast-food product and is not suitable for vegetarian, vegan, or halal diets.
common_user_queries: What is in the SmokeShack burger?
How many calories does a SmokeShack have?
Is the SmokeShack suitable for diabetics?
How much sodium is in a SmokeShack?
What is the glycemic index of a SmokeShack burger?
Where can I get a SmokeShack in Singapore?
What is the difference between a ShackBurger and a SmokeShack?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from USDA Branded Foods for Shake Shack SmokeShack (FDC ID 2341827), which is an approved secondary global source for commercial products. The match is exact for the product name and brand. GI is an analogous estimate as no direct GI measurement for this specific product exists in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature; the estimation basis is documented. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
